merged_findings: Backward packet length features consistently exhibit substantial class-conditioned structural variation characterized by high variance ratios and unique value disparities between BENIGN and DDoS classes.
Distributional shifts in Bwd Packet Length Mean, Std, and Max are strongly confirmed, supporting class-conditioned variation.

Moderate shortcut signals and local label-topology conflicts around key backward packet length features suggest potential labeling or sampling biases and unresolved local neighborhood inconsistencies.
preserved_contradictions: Persistent local label neighborhood conflicts and duplicate label conflicts in backward packet length features.
open_gaps: No direct distribution verification in task_1 limits deeper distributional insights.
No duplication verification performed in task_3 limits assessment of exact data duplication effects on bias.
Local label-topology conflicts remain unresolved within bounded neighborhoods in task_4.
No further bounded evidence acquisition possible within budget to resolve conflicts.
update_focus: Consolidate class-conditioned structural variation evidence while preserving unresolved local label conflicts and potential shortcut biases.
